---
title: "Introduction to Mplus and `MplusAutomation`"
author: "AERA 2026 Workshop"
date: "Last Updated: `r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

# Introduction to `MplusAutomation` {.tabset}

```{r}
#update.packages(ask = FALSE, checkBuilt = TRUE)
#install.packages(c("officer", "flextable"))
#restartSession()  # RStudio: Session > Restart R
```

# Read in full LSAY data set and subset

```{r}
# Select variables from raw dataset
#data_raw <- read_sav( here("data", "ICPSR_30263", "DS0001", "30263-0001-Data.sav"), user_na =TRUE)
#
#lsay_select <- data_raw %>%
#  select(CASENUM, COHORT, RSTEMM, 
#         GA32A, GA32H, GA32I, GA32K, GA32L, GA33A, GA33H, GA33I, GA33K, GA33L, # 10th grade
#         KA46A, KA46H, KA46I, KA46K, KA46L, KA47A, KA47H, KA47I, KA47K, KA47L) # 12th grade
#
##view_df(lsay_select)
#
#write_csv(lsay_select, here("data","lsay_cleaned.csv"))
```

# Read in .csv

```{r}
lsay_csv <- read_csv(here("data", "lsay_cleaned.csv")) %>% 
  mutate(
    across(c(-CASENUM, COHORT), ~ if_else(.x %in% c(-99, -98, -96, -95), NA_real_, .x))
  )
```

GA32A = I enjoy math - fall 10th grade

GA32H = Math useful in everyday problems - fall 10th grade

GA32I = Math helps logical thinking - fall 10th grade

GA32K = Need math for a good job - fall 10th grade

GA32L = Will use math often as an adult - fall 10th grade

GA33A = I enjoy science - fall 10th grade

GA33H = Science useful in everyday problems - fall 10th grade

GA33I = Science helps logical thinking - fall 10th grade

GA33K = Need science for a good job - fall 10th grade

GA33L = Will use science often as an adult - fall 10th grade

Response options:

1 - Strongly AGree 2 - Agree 3 - Not Sure 4 - Disagree 5 - Strongly Disagree

```{r}
table(as.numeric(lsay_csv$RSTEMM), useNA = "ifany")

psych::describe(lsay_csv)
summary(lsay_csv)
```

# Recode variables

```{r}
lsay_cleaned <- lsay_csv %>% 
  mutate(across(starts_with(c("GA","KA")), 
                ~ case_when(
                  .x %in% c(1, 2)    ~ 1,  
                  .x %in% c(3, 4, 5) ~ 0, 
                  TRUE               ~ NA_real_
                ))) %>% 
  filter(COHORT == 2) %>% # Filter Cohort 2 (N = 3116)
  filter(RSTEMM %in% c(0, 1)) # Filter to follow-up sample 

psych::describe(lsay_cleaned)
summary(lsay_cleaned)
```

# Descriptive Statistics

```{r}
# Define survey questions
all_questions <- c(
  "GA32A", "GA32H", "GA32I", "GA32K", "GA32L", "GA33A", "GA33H", "GA33I", "GA33K", "GA33L", # 10th grade
  "KA46A", "KA46H", "KA46I", "KA46K", "KA46L", "KA47A", "KA47H", "KA47I", "KA47K", "KA47L"  # 12th grade
)

# Function to compute stats (count, mean, SD, range)
compute_stats <- function(data, question, grade, question_name) {
  data %>%
    summarise(
      Grade = grade,
      Count = sum(!is.na(.data[[question]])),
      Mean = mean(.data[[question]], na.rm = TRUE),
      SD = sd(.data[[question]], na.rm = TRUE),
      Min = min(.data[[question]], na.rm = TRUE),
      Max = max(.data[[question]], na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    mutate(Question = question_name)
}

# Define question names and mappings
table_setup <- tibble(
  question_code = all_questions,
  grade = rep(c(10, 12), each = 10),
  question_name = rep(
    c(
      "I enjoy math",
      "Math is useful in everyday problems",
      "Math helps a person think logically",
      "It is important to know math to get a good job",
      "I will use math in many ways as an adult",
      "I enjoy science",
      "Science is useful in everyday problems",
      "Science helps a person think logically",
      "It is important to know science to get a good job",
      "I will use science in many ways as an adult"
    ),
    times = 2
  )
)

# Compute stats for all questions
table1_data <- pmap_dfr(
  list(table_setup$question_code, table_setup$grade, table_setup$question_name),
  ~compute_stats(lsay_cleaned, ..1, ..2, ..3)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Mean = round(Mean, 2),
    SD = round(SD, 2),
    Min = round(Min, 2),
    Max = round(Max, 2)
  ) %>%
  arrange(match(Question, table_setup$question_name), Grade) %>%
  select(Question, Grade, Count, Mean, SD, Min, Max)

# Build table
table1_gt <- table1_data %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "Question") %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Table 1. Descriptive Statistics for Mathematics and Science Attitudinal Survey Items Included in Analyses"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    Grade = "Grade",
    Count = "N",
    Mean = "M",
    SD = "SD",
    Min = "Min",
    Max = "Max"
  ) %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(Mean, SD, Min, Max),
    decimals = 2
  )

# Show table
table1_gt
```

# 10th Grade Enumeration

Enumeration

```{r, eval = FALSE}
lca_6  <- lapply(1:6, function(k) {
  lca_enum  <- mplusObject(
      
    TITLE = glue("{k}-Class"), 
  
    VARIABLE = glue(
    "categorical = GA32A - GA33L; 
     usevar = GA32A - GA33L; 
     classes = c({k}); "),
  
  ANALYSIS = 
   "estimator = mlr; 
    type = mixture;
    starts = 500 100; 
    processors = 10;",
  
  OUTPUT = "sampstat residual tech11 tech14;",
  
  usevariables = colnames(lsay_cleaned),
  rdata = lsay_cleaned)

lca_enum_fit <- mplusModeler(lca_enum, glue(here("lta_analysis","enumeration","t1","lsay_lta.dat")),
                            modelout= glue(here("lta_analysis","enumeration","t1","c{k}_lta.inp")),
                            check=TRUE, run = TRUE, hashfilename = FALSE)
})
```

Model Fit Summary

```{r}
source(here("functions", "extract_mplus_info.R"))
source(here("functions","enum_table.R"))

# Define the directory where all of the .out files are located.
output_dir <- here("lta_analysis","enumeration", "t1")

# Get all .out files
output_files <- list.files(output_dir, pattern = "\\.out$", full.names = TRUE)

# Process all .out files into one dataframe
final_data <- map_dfr(output_files, extract_mplus_info_extended)

# Extract Sample_Size from final_data
sample_size <- unique(final_data$Sample_Size)

output_enum_t1 <- readModels(here("lta_analysis","enumeration", "t1"), quiet = TRUE)
enum_table_full(output_enum_t1)
```

IC Plot

```{r}
source(here("functions","ic_plot_lca.R"))
ic_plot(output_enum_t1)
```

Plot LCA:

```{r, fig.width=20, fig.height= 7}
source(here("functions","plot_lca.R"))

t1 <- plot_lca(output_enum_t1$c4_lta.out)
t1
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![](figures/UCSB_Navy_mark.png)
